systems.name: Melodic Narrative System (Jazz as Language)
systems.summary: Bernstein's load-bearing conceptual system: improvisation is language use, not scale deployment. Every concept is anchored to a specific tune rather than to an abstract harmonic exercise; the standard is a poem and the melody is its story. The system's central aesthetic - connected narrative outranks correctness-of-notes - unifies vocabulary acquisition (by listening and imitation), the practice unit (small phrases, not transcribed choruses), and the evaluative criterion for any played line (does it tell a story?). Contrast, silence, and rhymes are the narrative grammar; target notes are the structural landing points around which lines are built.

members.name: Tune-as-Poem
members.summary: Each standard treated as a poem whose syntax, word-assembly, and rhymes the player is studying.

members.name: Melody-as-Story
members.summary: Within the poem, the melody is the story itself; harmony and ornamentation are supporting customs.

members.name: Phrase as Unit of Vocabulary
members.summary: Small phrases (not full transcribed choruses) are the practice and recombination unit.

members.name: Target Note
members.summary: The melody's own landing points on chord tones - the structural basis for improvised lines.

members.name: Contrast
members.summary: Soft against loud, short against long, serious against lighthearted - the engine of narrative engagement.

members.name: Silence
members.summary: Spaces between notes serve as punctuation between ideas.

members.name: Rhyme
members.summary: Motifs connected across time via call-and-response or parallel tension-resolution.

traversal_rules.name: Build Lines That Lead to Melody's Target Notes
traversal_rules.summary: Identify the melody's target notes and build improvised lines that lead to them, starting simply and growing in complexity.

references.quote_excerpt: build melodic lines that lead to them, starting simply
the melody's own target notes are the basis for improvisation

traversal_rules.name: Connected Narrative Outranks Correctness of Notes
traversal_rules.summary: Prefer ideas that relate to each other with purpose over notes that merely work over the chords.

references.quote_excerpt: ideas that relate to each other with purpose

traversal_rules.name: Contrast as Narrative Engine
traversal_rules.summary: Use contrast - soft against loud, short against long, serious against lighthearted - to sustain narrative engagement across a solo.

references.quote_excerpt: soft against loud, short against long, serious against lighthearted

traversal_rules.name: Withhold Dense Subdivisions Until Late
traversal_rules.summary: Pace dense rhythmic subdivisions to be withheld early and deployed later to build anticipation.

references.quote_excerpt: withholds dense subdivisions early and deploys them later

traversal_rules.name: Silence as Punctuation Between Ideas
traversal_rules.summary: Treat silences as punctuation between ideas, not as gaps to be filled.

references.quote_excerpt: the linguistic role of silence as punctuation between ideas
rhythm and the spaces between notes are what convert

traversal_rules.name: Rhymes Connect Motifs Across Time
traversal_rules.summary: Use rhymes - call-and-response or parallel tension-resolution - to link motifs across the form.

references.quote_excerpt: connecting motifs across time through call-and-response

modification_rules.name: Every Concept Anchored to a Specific Tune
modification_rules.summary: No concept is presented as an abstract system; every idea is anchored to a specific tune.

references.quote_excerpt: almost never explains a concept without anchoring it to a specific tune
all ideas are presented inside specific tunes

modification_rules.name: Cadenzas Require Definitive Endings
modification_rules.summary: End cadenzas with definitive, unambiguous endings under the performer's control (parking-a-car, ending-a-conversation analogies).

references.quote_excerpt: definitive, unambiguous endings under the performer's control

systems.name: Melody-Primary Improvisation System
systems.summary: Codifies the method's load-bearing pedagogical rule: melody is primary and harmony exists to serve it. The order-of-operations is explicit - the first practice task on any tune is to play the melody as a monophonic instrument, with attention to lyric syllables as a guide to articulation, before any harmonic embellishment. Target notes are the operational link between the melody and the harmony: when the melody lands on a specific chord tone, the player notes it and lets it dictate voicing choice. The 'juggling on a unicycle' maxim warns against texture-overload at the cost of melodic feel.

members.name: Monophonic Melody Delivery
members.summary: Play the tune as a single line with attention to lyric syllables.

members.name: Conservative Shell Voicing
members.summary: Root-3-7 shell voicings as the chord layer added after melody mastery.

members.name: Extension and Signature Run Layer
members.summary: Extensions and signature runs added only after the melody-chord relationship is heard.

members.name: Melody Target Note
members.summary: The specific chord tone the melody lands on; dictates voicing choice.

traversal_rules.name: Step 1 Before Step 12
traversal_rules.summary: Complete melody mastery before any harmonic embellishment; do not rush to step 12 before step 1.

references.quote_excerpt: must not rush to step 12 before completing step 1
play the melody as a monophonic instrument

traversal_rules.name: Enter With Conservative Shell Voicings
traversal_rules.summary: On solo guitar, enter with conservative shell voicings and only then expand to extensions and signature runs.

references.quote_excerpt: enters with conservative shell voicings

modification_rules.name: Target Note Dictates Voicing Choice
modification_rules.summary: When the melody lands on a specific chord tone, that note dictates voicing choice (e.g., Dm6 vs Dm7 chosen by whether the melody is the 6 or the b7).

references.quote_excerpt: the melody lands on a specific chord tone and lets it dictate voicing choice
the melody's b7 overrides the default minor-tonic Dm6

modification_rules.name: Place Chords Only in Spaces the Melody Allows
modification_rules.summary: Chords are placed in the spaces the melody allows, never the reverse; never sacrifice melody phrasing for harmony.

references.quote_excerpt: chords are placed in the spaces the melody allows
warns against texture-overload at the cost of melodic feel

modification_rules.name: Memorize Tensions by Sensory Flavor
modification_rules.summary: Tensions are memorized by sensory flavor rather than symbol manipulation; a single pitch carries different flavors under different chords.

references.quote_excerpt: tensions are memorized by sensory flavor rather than symbol manipulation

references.topic: A note's flavor depends on harmony
references.quote_excerpt: a single pitch carries different flavors under different chords

systems.name: Comping Aesthetic System (Dressing the Notes)
systems.summary: Applies the melody-first hierarchy to accompaniment via the signature 'dressing the notes' metaphor: comping's job is to give each melody note the right outfit so it feels important. The concrete practice rule is to begin with root-3-7 voicings only, internalize their relationship to the melody, and add extensions only after that relationship is heard. The chapter prescribes a two-phase comping-construction procedure - design a guide-tone line per chord as a secondary melody, then fill the inner voices to dress those guide tones. Tension and resolution are generated even inside a single sustained chord, and rhythm in comping is elevated to equal status with voicing choice.

members.name: Root-3-7 Shell Voicing
members.summary: The basic chord layer that establishes the melody-chord relationship before extensions are added.

members.name: Guide-Tone Line
members.summary: A self-designed melodic line through each chord, functioning as a secondary melody under the tune.

members.name: Inner-Voice Dressing
members.summary: Inner voices filled around the guide-tone line to dress those guide tones.

members.name: Extension-as-Outfit (e.g., B7#11)
members.summary: A tension chosen so the melody note becomes a color rather than a doubling.

members.name: Rhythmic Motif
members.summary: A borrowed or developed rhythmic cell (e.g., Star Eyes intro) used as a developable comping unit.

traversal_rules.name: Foundation Before Embellishment
traversal_rules.summary: Begin with root-3-7 voicings and add extensions only after the relationship between basic chords and melody is internalized.

references.quote_excerpt: begin with root-3-7 voicings only, internalize the relationship
a layered foundation-before-embellishment discipline

traversal_rules.name: Two-Phase Comping Construction
traversal_rules.summary: First design your own guide-tone line per chord as a secondary melody; then fill the inner voices to dress those guide tones.

references.quote_excerpt: design your own guide-tone line per chord as a secondary melody
fill the inner voices to dress those guide tones

traversal_rules.name: Comping Voicing Tops Mirror Solo Target Notes
traversal_rules.summary: The top voicings used while comping are drawn from the same melodic target notes used as solo landing points - one system covers two roles.

references.quote_excerpt: drawn from the same melodic target notes he uses as solo landing points

traversal_rules.name: Internal Tension/Resolution Within a Single Chord
traversal_rules.summary: Generate internal tension and resolution even when a single chord is sustained over multiple measures; locate creative work in tension zones rather than at resolutions.

references.topic: Internal motion within a single chord
references.quote_excerpt: generate internal tension/resolution even when a single chord is sustained
locate creative work in tension zones rather than at resolutions

traversal_rules.name: Bend Groove to the Soloist
traversal_rules.summary: The comper bends groove to the soloist's reality rather than the reverse.

references.quote_excerpt: bends groove to the soloist's reality rather than the reverse

modification_rules.name: Dress the Notes
modification_rules.summary: Choose each voicing so the melody note becomes an important color, not a weak doubling (e.g., substitute B7#11 so the melody is the tension rather than the doubled bass).

references.quote_excerpt: give each melody note the right outfit so it feels important
the melody note becomes a tension rather than being weakly doubled

modification_rules.name: Rhythm Equal to Voicing in Comping
modification_rules.summary: Rhythm in comping is as load-bearing as voicing; borrow rhythmic motifs (e.g., Parker's 'Star Eyes' intro) as developable units.

references.quote_excerpt: rhythm is elevated to equal status with voicing choice
borrowing rhythmic motifs such as the intro of Parker's Star Eyes

modification_rules.name: Reharmonize to Anticipate the Soloist's Melody
modification_rules.summary: Extend comping into real-time reharmonization that anticipates the soloist's notes while preserving the song's harmonic narrative.

references.quote_excerpt: real-time reharmonization that anticipates the soloist's notes

modification_rules.name: Under-Resolve the I Chord
modification_rules.summary: Avoid overemphasizing the I chord; substitute a V-derived I69 to keep tension unresolved over the tonic and avoid the root of the I chord.

references.topic: Avoid the root of the I chord
references.quote_excerpt: substituting a V-derived I69 to keep tension unresolved
the forward-motion rule of under-resolving the I chord

modification_rules.name: Tritone-Distance ii-V Over a Resolving Dominant
modification_rules.summary: Insert a ii-V at the tritone distance over a resolving dominant; rhythmic placement of the substitution matters as much as its harmonic logic.

references.quote_excerpt: inserting a tritone-distance ii-V over a resolving dominant
rhythmic placement of the substitution matters as much

modification_rules.name: Voice Leading as Choir Writing
modification_rules.summary: Treat each string as a singer in a choir, giving each line a beautiful melodic shape (Jim Hall framing).

references.quote_excerpt: each string is a singer in a choir to be given a beautiful melodic line

systems.name: Vocabulary Development System (Recursive Variation and Target-Note Practice)
systems.summary: The apparatus by which a player expands the internalized library of phrases that improvisation recombines. Vocabulary is acquired the way children learn to speak - by listening and imitation - and creativity is defined operationally as recursive variation: ten ideas from one, then ten from each of those. The chapter enumerates three named variation methods (ear-led free variation, phrases built around a single target note, phrases around two simultaneous target notes) and pairs them with a permissive chromatic stance under the 'party for the target' analogy: diatonic notes are family, chromatic notes are friends and neighbors. The system closes with two practice meta-rules - practice away from the instrument so imagination leads the fingers, and learn classic lines first then progressively abstract them.

members.name: Ear-Led Free Variation
members.summary: Variation that preserves the line's overall structure while changing notes by ear.

members.name: Single Target-Note Variation
members.summary: Phrases built around a single chosen target note from the chord.

members.name: Two Simultaneous Target Notes
members.summary: Phrases built around two target notes at once.

members.name: Diatonic 'Family' Notes
members.summary: Diatonic notes around a target - the 'family' in the party-for-the-target analogy.

members.name: Chromatic 'Friends and Neighbors'
members.summary: Chromatic notes that can approach any target if they serve the line.

members.name: Classic Line
members.summary: Canonical phrase learned by imitation, the starting unit for abstraction.

traversal_rules.name: Recursive Variation: Ten From One
traversal_rules.summary: Generate ten variations from a single idea, then ten more from each of those - creativity defined as recursive variation generation.

references.quote_excerpt: ten ideas from one, then ten from each of those

traversal_rules.name: Open (Ear-Led) Variation Preserving Structure
traversal_rules.summary: Generate ear-led free variations that preserve the line's overall structure.

references.quote_excerpt: ear-led free variation that preserves the line's overall structure

traversal_rules.name: Phrases Built Around a Target Note
traversal_rules.summary: Build phrases that orbit a single chosen chord-tone target.

references.topic: Target notes around a chord tone
references.quote_excerpt: phrases built around a chosen target note from the chord

traversal_rules.name: Two-Note Target Practice
traversal_rules.summary: Build phrases around two simultaneous target notes.

references.quote_excerpt: phrases built around two simultaneous target notes

traversal_rules.name: Shape-Based Idea Generation
traversal_rules.summary: Play what you see inside a fingerboard shape and evaluate by ear, distinguishing intentional phrasing from 'pressing the buttons'.

references.quote_excerpt: playing what you see inside a fingerboard shape and then evaluating by ear
distinguishing intentional phrasing from pressing the buttons

modification_rules.name: Party for the Target (Any Note Can Approach Any Target)
modification_rules.summary: Permissive chromatic stance: diatonic notes are family, chromatic notes are friends and neighbors, so any note can approach any target if it serves the line.

references.quote_excerpt: diatonic notes are family and chromatic notes are friends and neighbors
any note can approach any target

modification_rules.name: Acquire Vocabulary by Listening and Imitation
modification_rules.summary: Acquire vocabulary the way children learn to speak - by listening and imitation, not from books; recombine known phrases under a syntax rather than generating tokens in real time.

references.quote_excerpt: the way children learn to speak by listening and imitation
recombining known phrases under a syntax rather than generating tokens

modification_rules.name: Tunes Outrank Solos as Vocabulary Source
modification_rules.summary: The composer's own ideas in the tune are the most concentrated vocabulary source, so tunes outrank transcribed solos; the practice unit is the small phrase, not the full chorus.

references.quote_excerpt: tunes outrank solos as the source of concentrated vocabulary
the practice unit is the small phrase, not the full transcribed chorus

modification_rules.name: Practice Away From the Instrument
modification_rules.summary: Sing ideas over the song's form away from the instrument so imagination leads the fingers - the 'proper chain of command'.

references.quote_excerpt: singing ideas over the song's form, establishing a proper chain of command

modification_rules.name: Learn Classic Lines, Then Progressively Abstract
modification_rules.summary: Learn classic lines first and then progressively abstract them into variations - impressionistic abstraction of the masters rather than literal reproduction.

references.quote_excerpt: learn classic lines first and then progressively abstract them
impressionistic abstraction of the great masters rather than literal reproduction

modification_rules.name: Fingering Serves Articulation
modification_rules.summary: Choose fingering to serve articulation rather than convenience; assign the strongest fingers (middle or ring) to melodic target notes for tonal weight.

references.quote_excerpt: fingering is chosen to serve articulation rather than convenience
strongest fingers (middle or ring) are assigned to melodic target notes

modification_rules.name: Feel Intervals on a Single String
modification_rules.summary: Play intervals on a single string with a subtle slide to emulate horn phrasing.

references.quote_excerpt: playing them on a single string with a subtle slide to emulate horn phrasing

modification_rules.name: One Shape, Moving Hand
modification_rules.summary: Maintain a single chord shape and shift the hand around the neck rather than reshaping fingers for each chord.

references.quote_excerpt: maintaining a single chord shape and shifting the hand around the neck
